interpretation_hypothesis: Tail-controlled PT amplitude (upper quantiles dominate kappa1)
evidence_FOR: Strong Pearson/Spearman kappa1 vs q90_I (~0.78/0.81); tail_width correlates ~0.66/0.62 (Agent 20A).
evidence_AGAINST: Flag KAPPA1_TAIL_DOMINATED=NO (|corr(q90)| not clearly above |corr(q50)|); best LOOCV uses tail_width + S_peak not q90 alone; tail_mass_quantile_top12p5 ~0.09 correlation; Agent 18B used different tail regressor—do not merge verdicts blindly.
notes: Use 'tail–bulk structure' language; single upper-quantile scalar is incomplete.

evidence_FOR: Switching module lists width and geometric observables; X links experiments.
evidence_AGAINST: No dedicated ridge-curvature vs kappa1 LOOCV or correlation surfaced in surveyed reports; ridge band metrics appear in composite scans not tied to kappa1 here.
notes: Treat as open measurement gap unless a named run is added.

evidence_FOR: Rank-1 term collapses strip LOOCV error vs PT-only (~79% relative improvement); mode-1 variance ~0.96 rank report.
evidence_AGAINST: kappa1 is simultaneously predictable from S_peak + width—'susceptibility' mixes barrier and peak observables.
notes: Good operational name if defined as rank-1 coefficient kappa1(T), not an independent susceptibility measurement.

evidence_AGAINST: kappa1 is the successful rank-1 fit weight; 'collapse' and failure modes attach more to rank-2 / alpha_res / 22 K anomalies.
notes: Do not equate kappa1 with residual failure metrics.

evidence_FOR: Linear model kappa1 ~ tail_width + S_peak with LOOCV RMSE ~0.0185 vs std(kappa1)~0.355; stable excluding 22–24 K.
evidence_AGAINST: High collinearity with S_peak (rho~0.97)—hard to isolate unique 'geometry' vs 'scale'.
notes: Strongest paper-safe statement: kappa1 is predictable from barrier spread and S_peak; interpret as mixed geometric–scale coordinate.
